checkpoint_identity.series_role: authoritative pre-relayout validation, decision, and packaging checkpoint for the completed repo-structure series; downstream of stronger canon, the six repo-structure packets, post-Zeta closure, ultra audit evidence, XStack/AIDE closure, and selected live implementation evidence
checkpoint_identity.replacement_target: later first-slice execution checkpoint or explicit replacement review after RMS-001 execution or new baseline-hardening evidence

candidate_next_work_under_review: RMS-001 - No-Move Path Authority And Wrapper-Precedence Normalization

series_completion_findings.title: constraints and current reality
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet freezes what must not break, preserves baseline-first priority, and keeps verified, likely, and blocked distinct.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: None beyond the already-frozen blocker set.

series_completion_findings.title: topology, paths, and ownership reality
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet maps the live root families, ownership classes, path contracts, and baseline-critical clusters with implementation evidence.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: Mixed roots remain intentionally mixed in the map; that is a feature, not a gap.

series_completion_findings.title: coupling, drift, and relayout risk
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet ranks safe-to-move, needs-shim, and do-not-move-yet surfaces clearly enough to constrain design and migration.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: The packet proves that physical movement beyond the lightest slice is still high risk pre-baseline.

series_completion_findings.title: topology option generation and comparison
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet generated materially distinct options and discriminated among them honestly instead of collapsing them into aesthetic ties.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: TTO-002 remains a useful long-term reference, but not the selected target.

series_completion_findings.title: preferred target and authoritative boundary model
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet selected PT-001 / TTO-001 for repo-grounded reasons and froze a usable boundary model for later migration planning.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: The selection is conceptual only and does not authorize physical relayout on its own.

series_completion_findings.title: migration, shims, validation, and rollback
series_completion_findings.why_decision_grade: The packet defines phases, shim classes, concrete slices, validation gates, rollback thresholds, and the safest first slice.
series_completion_findings.active_caution: Only RMS-001 is safe pre-baseline; later phases remain gated on playable-baseline proof.

why_still_preferred: it best preserves the verify lane, FAST validation path, Python/AppShell launcher and setup shells, SessionX create/boot path, current saves/<save_id> assumptions, and the loopback-authoritative local path
it matches the current live root federation and does not invent new physical owners that the code does not actually honor today
it aligns strongly with extend-over-replace doctrine and XStack/AIDE non-interference
it leaves mixed or split ownership surfaces visible instead of pretending they are already settled
critical_uncertainties_still_unresolved: one canonical repo-local playtest command still does not exist
server/server_main.py repo-root resolution remains blocked truth
session_boot and sessionx/runner.py still disagree about save-root generality
schema versus schemas and packs versus data/packs remain deferred ownership splits
the authored MVP baseline trio and the live default bundle id still form an implementation-versus-guidance seam

first_migration_slice_findings.approved_slice_title: No-Move Path Authority And Wrapper-Precedence Normalization
first_migration_slice_findings.approval_scope: MP-0 doc-and-mirror-only normalization; no runtime, entrypoint, manifest, import, include, or path-logic change is approved
why_safe_first: it changes no imports, includes, or runtime path logic
it does not touch Omega2 do-not-move-yet surfaces directly
it targets the exact drift identified by the audit and Omega2 packet: stale src path vocabulary, wrapper overclaims, and ambiguous path-authority narratives
rollback is trivial compared with any slice that would touch AppShell, SessionX, loopback authority, release/trust, or build-path ownership
validation_must_immediately_follow: confirm prose and machine-readable mirrors still align
confirm that the slice did not touch runtime, entrypoint, or path-resolution code
immediate_rollback_triggers: source-of-truth ambiguity increases instead of decreasing
a doc or mirror starts claiming a different canonical entrypoint than the live shells and audit evidence support
scope expands into runtime behavior, imports, includes, manifests, session semantics, or real path logic
report or audit regeneration begins to misstate runtime truth

residual_risk_ledger.title: canonical playable-baseline path is still not hardened
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Blocks MP-1 and all later slices that touch entrypoints or product-shell seams.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: Does not block RMS-001, but forbids using RMS-001 as proof that broader relayout is now safe.

residual_risk_ledger.title: server/server_main.py repo-root misresolution
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Keeps direct Python server startup blocked and makes local-authority seam movement unsafe.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: Must stay documented truth; RMS-001 must not overclaim readiness.

residual_risk_ledger.title: session_boot and sessionx/runner.py save-root coupling
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Keeps session-location movement and generated-output rebinding unsafe.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: RMS-001 may document the constraint, not fix it.

residual_risk_ledger.title: launcher supervision instability
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Keeps AppShell and operator-shell relayout high risk.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: RMS-001 must not describe supervision as solved.

residual_risk_ledger.title: native launcher/setup and non-loopback surfaces remain stub-heavy
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Prevents wrappers from being treated as canonical owners.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: RMS-001 should narrow wrapper readiness claims, not widen them.

residual_risk_ledger.title: stale src mirrors plus tools/import_bridge.py still coexist
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Drift is real and is exactly why the first slice exists, but bridge retirement remains later work.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: This is the main risk addressed by RMS-001.

residual_risk_ledger.title: default bundle seam between authored MVP guidance and bundle.base.lab
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: Silent normalization would create false truth during later movement or path cleanup.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: RMS-001 must keep the seam visible.

residual_risk_ledger.title: split-root ambiguity in schema/schemas and packs/data-packs
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: RMS-001 must not pick winners by wording.

residual_risk_ledger.title: validation strength after a doc-only slice is necessarily narrow
residual_risk_ledger.why_it_matters: FAST proves consistency and non-regression in the repo audit pipeline, but not playable-baseline readiness.
residual_risk_ledger.effect_on_first_slice: Requires an explicit stop after RMS-001; no automatic progression to RMS-002.

why: the series is complete and decision-grade
RMS-001 is a real execution slice with meaningful value, not a redundant extra clarification artifact
RMS-001 does not compete with the baseline-first priority because it is no-move, no-shim, and no-runtime-change
broader relayout execution is still not safe because the canonical playable-baseline path is not yet hardened

ordered_steps: Immediately validate the slice with JSON parse for touched mirrors, prose/mirror consistency, git diff --check, and python tools/validation/tool_run_validation.py --profile FAST.
Stop after the slice and record MP-0 complete while broader relayout remains gated.
Return the broader product priority to canonical playable-baseline hardening and its blockers.
Do not attempt RMS-002, MP-1, any physical relayout, any shim introduction, split-root convergence, or any XStack/AIDE-shaped extraction work yet.

forbidden_execution_shapes: treating RMS-001 as permission to start physical relayout immediately after merge
slipping runtime, entrypoint, manifest, or path-resolution edits into a doc-normalization slice
using stale audit or doc cleanup to overstate readiness
bundling RMS-001 with baseline-blocker fixes and calling the combined work cleanup
treating the preferred conceptual target as if the repo had already migrated
